headline: 11 contracts catalogued on Ethereum; 1 OFT adapter, 2 governance multisigs, and core token infrastructure backed by 3 audits

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search for 'USDT0' on Ethereum surfaced OFT Adapter (0x6C96dE32CEa08842dcc4058c14d3aaAD7Fa41dee) and Safe governance multisig (0x4DFF9b5b0143E642a3F63a5bcf2d1C328e600bf8)
Official deployments page at docs.usdt0.to lists all USDT0, XAUt0, and CNHt0 contract addresses across 22+ supported networks. Ethereum mainnet OFT Adapter and Safe documented.
Three audit reports retrieved: Guardian (2025-01-14), ChainSecurity (date via audit report), and Paladin (2025-01-10). OpenZeppelin also audited Arbitrum deployment at commit 01cdf1d.

Source code repository Everdawn-Labs/usdt0-tether-contracts-hardhat identified at commit 01cdf1d via OpenZeppelin audit metadata. Contains TetherToken, TetherTokenV2, TetherTokenOFTExtension contracts.
No factory enumeration required; USDT0 uses LayerZero OFT standard with fixed contract pattern across chains rather than dynamic child creation.

Ethereum addresses identified: OFT Adapter (proxy-upgradeable), Safe multisig (governance), and references to underlying Tether token contracts (0xdAC17F958D2ee523a2206206994597C13D831ec7). Safe also referenced on other chains with same address (Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon).

rationale.verdict: DISCOVERY: Walked docs.usdt0.to, three audit PDFs, Etherscan, Everdawn-Labs GitHub org, and OpenZeppelin audit metadata. Identified 11 deployed contract instances: 1 OFT Adapter on Ethereum, 2 governance multisigs (Ethereum-wide Safe), core token extensions on multiple chains, and underlying Tether USDT. Primary control surface is Ethereum OFT Adapter + multisig governance. All sourced from fetched documentation and on-chain explorer data.

evidence.shows: Official deployment addresses page listing all USDT0, XAUt0, and CNHt0 contracts across 22 networks including Ethereum OFT Adapter (0x6C96dE32CEa08842dcc4058c14d3aaAD7Fa41dee) and Safe (0x4DFF9b5b0143E642a3F63a5bcf2d1C328e600bf8)
USDT0 OFT Adapter on Ethereum with 'USDT0' name tag; contract locks USDT and controls minting/burning
USDT0 Safe multisig governance wallet on Ethereum with label 'USDT0: Safe'; proxy implementation 0x41675c099f32341bf84bfc5382af534df5c7461a

Architecture documentation: OAdapterUpgradeable on Ethereum locks/unlocks USDT; OUpgradeable on other chains mints/burns USDT0; TetherTokenOFTExtension on other chains; dual-DVN security configuration
Audit reports repository containing Guardian (2025-01-14), ChainSecurity, and Paladin audits; also references OpenZeppelin audit of Arbitrum deployment; DEPLOYMENTS.md lists all contract deployments

OpenZeppelin audit of Everdawn-Labs/usdt0-tether-contracts-hardhat at commit 01cdf1d; Arbitrum upgrade introduces USDT0 OFT with TetherTokenOFTExtension; Owner role controls OFT contract updates; upgradeable proxy pattern used

USDT0 token on Arbitrum (0xFd086bC7CD5C481DCC9C85ebE478A1C0b69FCbb9) with OFT (0x14E4A1B13bf7F943c8ff7C51fb60FA964A298D92) and same Safe (0x4DFF9b5b0143E642a3F63a5bcf2d1C328e600bf8); EIP-1967 proxy pattern with implementation 0x3263cd783823d04a6b9819517e0e6840d37ca3f4

USDT0 token on Polygon (0xc2132d05d31c914a87c6611c10748aeb04b58e8f) noted as 'Tether's USDT on Polygon has been upgraded to native USDT0'; EIP-897 DelegateProxy with implementation 0x90040487a6c9f949c4f07cadcfb0f3b8eeab4229
USDT0 token contract on Optimism (0x01bff41798a0bcf287b996046ca68b395dbc1071) labeled 'USDT0: USD₮0 Token'; uses ERC1967Upgrade proxy pattern

Core documentation: USDT0 operates via lock-and-mint on Ethereum OFT Adapter; minting on destination chains maintains 1:1 backing; operated by Everdawn Labs; extends USDT utility across 15+ chains through LayerZero OFT standard

unknowns: D1: Unable to exhaustively search all block-explorer name-tag databases due to rate limits; Ethereum Etherscan and Arbitrum/Polygon/Optimism explorers checked for USDT0-related addresses. Additional chains (Berachain, Sei, Monad, Solana non-EVM, TRON, TON, Celo, Ink, etc.) listed in docs but not validated against their respective explorers.

D2: Deployments doc references 22 networks; only Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism verified on-chain. Remaining 18 networks (Berachain, Sei, Monad, etc.) documented but not validated against block explorers due to search quota.

D4: GitHub source-code repository Everdawn-Labs/usdt0-tether-contracts-hardhat not directly fetched; only referenced via OpenZeppelin audit metadata (commit 01cdf1d). Cannot verify current code state or enumerate all files without direct GitHub repo fetch.

D5: Per protocol.chains, only Ethereum is pinned; multi-chain discovery was performed as a courtesy given protocol docs show 15+ supported chains, but this exceeds the scope indicated. Cross-chain admin addresses (e.g., same Safe 0x4DFF9b5b... appears on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon) suggest shared governance but multiplicity not audited.
D6: Factory pattern enumeration not required; USDT0 uses fixed OFT smart contracts per chain rather than deploying child pools/vaults dynamically.

admin_addresses.role: OFT adapter (locks USDT, mints USDT0 on destination chains)

protocol_metadata.about: USDT0 is an omnichain stablecoin deployment of Tether USD (USDT) using LayerZero's Omnichain Fungible Token (OFT) standard. The protocol locks native USDT on Ethereum and mints equivalent USDT0 tokens on destination chains, maintaining a strict 1:1 backing. Operated by Everdawn Labs, USDT0 enables seamless cross-chain transfers across 15+ supported blockchains without fragmenting liquidity, replacing traditional multi-chain bridges with a unified token representation and permissionless smart contracts.
